briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the daily climate change briefing for June 19, 2026.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Negotiations and Domestic Participation
sections.content: The Republic of Korea government actively participated in the preparatory meetings and subsidiary body meetings for the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) Conference of the Parties (COP), discussing implementation strategies for its Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) [1, 4, 14, 15, 20]. Key implementation measures of the Paris Agreement, such as clean energy transition, power grid modernization, and climate finance mobilization, were intensively discussed at these meetings [5, 7, 9, 17]. Notably, Korea demonstrated its contribution to the international community by sharing its case study on establishing Nationally Determined Contributions at a climate conference held in Germany [19]. However, the international community faces the reality that UN summits alone cannot solve climate change through dialogue [95], and significant challenges remain, such as the United States being criticized for climate change controversies at an Antarctic conference [108]. Amidst this, movements to pursue a major transformation in domestic carbon neutrality governance are also being observed, through the establishment of a Climate Citizens' Assembly and the launch of the National Institute of Climate Science [10].

sections.title: 🔴 Intensifying Climate Crisis and Warnings
sections.content: Climate-related news reported today warns that the climate crisis is intensifying globally. Flooding in desert regions [3] and extreme weather events in Canada [112] illustrate the reality of abnormal climate patterns, and ecosystem damage is also severe, with Australian native bees directly hit by heatwaves [8]. According to research, climate change is linked to stunted growth in African children [96], increased mycotoxin contamination in European wheat [99], and a decline in the quantity and quality of major US food products [111], raising concerns about food security. In particular, the accelerated melting of Arctic sea ice and changes in Mongolia's permafrost are clear signals of the instability of the global climate system [114, 115]. Furthermore, societal indifference to climate change [109], the spread of misinformation on social media [102], and the domestic situation where amendments to the Carbon Neutrality Act are delayed despite the urgent need for 'decarbonization' [48] further emphasize the urgency of responding to the climate crisis.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Transition
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are accelerating. The expansion of greenhouse gas statistical scope, the establishment of the Climate Citizens' Assembly, and the launch of the National Institute of Climate Science are signals of governance changes aligned with the international climate system transition [2, 10]. At the local government level, Cheongsong County has successfully reduced residents' carbon emissions through its Carbon Neutrality Point system [22, 31], while Chungju City and Seongnam City are gaining attention for their citizen-led carbon-neutral green city initiatives and administrative models [65, 66]. In the education sector, Maeseong Middle School and Maeseong High School are successfully leading student-driven carbon neutrality practice projects, expanding practical education [25, 30, 34, 40, 42, 50]. Furthermore, concrete measures to achieve carbon neutrality across industry and daily life are being explored, including policy discussions for eco-friendly railway transition [26, 43, 47, 55, 62] and the introduction of AI-managed carbon-neutral accommodations [24, 58, 60, 64].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Technology Investment
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is a global trend, with active investment and technological development in this area. In the United States, large-scale infrastructure development is showing results, with the largest renewable energy project in history completed in 16 years, supplying power to 1 million households [84, 116]. Domestically, CNPlus announced business strengthening by signing an MOU with global renewable energy investment firm Equal Energy, covering all new and renewable energy sectors, including wind, solar, and ESS [71, 73, 75, 89]. Furthermore, renewable energy financial support has been reformed, establishing grounds for supporting 'Sunshine Income Villages' [72], and the role of the financial sector is expanding, with Hana Bank arranging project financing for offshore wind power [81]. The Korea Power Exchange is making all-out efforts to build a stable supply chain for renewable energy to prevent a power crisis this summer [79], and the acceleration of solar power transition in Southeast Asia is expected to provide new opportunities for Korea's renewable energy and power grid technologies [85].

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Building
sections.content: Efforts to adapt to the impacts of climate change and strengthen resilience are also progressing in various ways. In the agricultural sector, new domestic varieties resilient to climate change are being selected to replace the 'Summi' potato [6], and a win-win fund for climate crisis response is being provided to farmers cultivating radish and cabbage in alpine regions [13], promoting the establishment of climate-resilient agricultural systems. Ongjin County is undertaking intensive control of sudden pest outbreaks to protect crops from abnormal climate conditions [18], and the Korea Agency for Infrastructure Safety and Technology is enhancing its facility inspection and diagnostic systems to prepare for climate change [16]. Internationally, deep-sea observation systems tracking climate change have averted dismantling [97], and new missions to explore oceanic responses to climate change are commencing [113], emphasizing the importance of scientific monitoring and research. Innovative adaptation strategies, both nature-based and technology-based, are also gaining attention, such as exploring solutions using plant roots [107] and ways mapping technology can contribute to climate change response [110].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, the climate crisis is intensifying across all fronts; however, international discussions and various domestic and global efforts to address it are also steadily continuing. This concludes today's climate briefing.
